title: Simple Rankett Fulfillment — the dead-simple breakdown of the business model + 3-tier
html:
Simple Rankett Fulfillment — the dead-simple breakdown of the business model + 3-tier

Source: 2026-02-07 Google Doc. The explainer-style breakdown of Rankett's business model + tier mechanics + monthly rhythm — likely written for partners or as internal alignment. Plain language, ASCII tables, cleaner than the more complex docs.

TL;DR

  • One-sentence what-it-does: "We put stuff about your client's business all over the internet so AI systems (ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity) find it and recommend them."
  • The 3-player money flow (with margins):
    • Client (dentist, lawyer, plumber) pays agency $2,500-$12,000/mo.
    • Agency keeps fat margin, pays Rankett $998-$2,498/mo.
    • Rankett does ALL the actual work.
    • Tier 1 agency profit: $1,500-$2,500/mo per client. Tier 3 agency profit: $7,500-$9,500/mo per client.
  • 3-tier matrix (who/charge/blogs/PR/forum/work-hours/clients-per-employee):
    • Tier 1 (local): $998/mo, 2 blogs, 3 PR, 0 forum, 8.5 hrs/mo, ~19 clients per employee.
    • Tier 2 (regional): $1,498/mo, 4 blogs, 7 PR, 3-5 forum, 21.5 hrs/mo, ~7 clients per employee.
    • Tier 3 (national): $2,498/mo, 8+ blogs, 15+ PR, 10+ forum, 44 hrs/mo, 3-4 clients per employee.
  • The 3 layers Rankett does:
    • Layer 1 — make AI able to read site: schema markup (translates to AI-readable), robots.txt (don't block crawlers), llms.txt (AI cheat sheet).
    • Layer 2 — give AI quotable content: answer-first blogs (1st paragraph = direct answer + statistic + owner quote = 67% more citations); YouTube audio (transcript-readable, 0.737 correlation with AI visibility); FAQ pages (Q&A 3.1× more quotable).
    • Layer 3 — prove legitimacy externally: review platforms (2.6-3.5× more citations), Reddit/Quora (40% of all LLM citations), PR mentions (61% of reputation signals from editorial), directory listings (85% of brand mentions from third-party).
  • Monthly rhythm: weeks 1-3 = create + distribute; week 4 = MEASURE everything + refresh AI visibility score + write monthly report.
  • 3 content rules for every blog: answer first (40-60 words, no fluff) + include statistic (+35-40% visibility) + include expert quote (+40% visibility).
  • What absolutely DOESN'T work: keyword stuffing (-8.3% visibility — makes it WORSE), AI-generated content, promised guaranteed AI rankings (citations change 40-60% monthly), volume over quality, "training data optimization."
  • Capacity economics: Tier 1 ~50%+ gross margin = profit engine; Tier 2 ~15-28% margin; Tier 3 often barely breaks even. Ideal portfolio: heavy Tier 1, some Tier 2, few Tier 3 for prestige.
  • Sample portfolio: 10 Tier 1 + 5 Tier 2 + 2 Tier 3 = ~$22,500/mo revenue with only 1.75 FTE.

Pattern observed

This is Yahir-as-explainer — the same simplification instinct that drives the 12 month plan but pushed even further. Plain language, no jargon, ASCII tables. Likely written to use with prospective partners during the partner meetup era.

The answer-first / statistic / expert-quote rule is the most distillable Rankett-IP in the corpus. Combined with the layer-1/2/3 framework (technical readability → quotable content → external trust), this doc would let any junior team member implement the playbook.

The economic note that Tier 3 barely breaks even is brutally honest internal accounting — most agencies wouldn't admit that. Suggests Yahir uses Tier 3 for credibility/case-studies, not margin.

Key quote

"The more places a business shows up with consistent, quality information, the more AI trusts and recommends them. Agencies resell this at 2-4x markup. That's the whole business."

Open questions

  • Has the 1.75-FTE portfolio model actually been achieved?
  • The 0.737 YouTube correlation with AI visibility — citation source?
  • Does Rankett currently have any Tier 3 clients, or is that aspirational?
